name: GPT
strengths: Reasoning, coding, tool use, multimodal work
weaknesses: Requires careful prompts and current verification
benchmarks: Strong general reasoning and coding performance
summary: GPT models are general-purpose AI systems used for chat, coding, analysis, automation, and multimodal assistants.

name: Claude
strengths: Writing, analysis, coding, long document reasoning
weaknesses: Model availability and pricing vary by tier
summary: Claude is a family of assistant models focused on helpfulness, safety, writing quality, and developer workflows.

name: Gemini
weaknesses: Best results depend on model and product surface
summary: Gemini powers Google AI products and supports text, code, image, audio, and video understanding.

name: Grok
weaknesses: Ecosystem is newer than some competitors
summary: Grok is xAI's model family designed for conversational AI and integrations across xAI and X products.

name: Llama
weaknesses: Requires engineering effort for production hosting
pricing: Open model licensing and hosting costs
summary: Llama models are widely used for open-source AI applications, private deployments, and research.

name: DeepSeek
summary: DeepSeek models are popular for code, reasoning, and cost-conscious AI experimentation.

name: Qwen
weaknesses: Production suitability depends on region and license
summary: Qwen is a broad model family used for chat, coding, multilingual applications, and multimodal tasks.

name: Mistral
strengths: Efficient models, European AI ecosystem, open options
weaknesses: Model choice matters for complex reasoning
summary: Mistral models are known for efficient deployment, strong open models, and enterprise-friendly AI products.

name: Phi
strengths: Small-model efficiency, local and edge use cases
weaknesses: Less capable than large frontier models
summary: Phi models are compact language models useful for local apps, education, and constrained hardware.

name: Command R
benchmarks: Strong retrieval and enterprise assistant focus
summary: Command R models are designed for enterprise AI, retrieval augmented generation, and knowledge workflows.
